velocity.method: Commit histories of makeready-iphone (277), makeready-server (366), makeready-web (658), makeready-capture (8) and the consolidated monorepo (241) were clustered into delivery epics. The 12-month plan assumes ~1.5 feature-epic-equivalents per month — conservative vs. the demonstrated 4.9 — to absorb verification, production hardening and unknowns.

budget.planCovers: Claude Max plan retained for tooling access only — development tokens are NOT offset (no Opus usage assumed)
budget.extraTokensModel: All development on Claude Fable 5, billed as extra tokens from 2026-07-07: $10/M input, $50/M output, ~$1/M cache reads, $12.50/M cache writes

measurement.source: Local Claude Code session transcripts (233 files, 13,501 assistant messages)
measurement.window: 2026-06-11 to 2026-07-05 (25 days; older transcripts not retained)

hybrid.note: Routine implementation routed to Max-plan-covered Opus 4.8 (historically ~69% of tokens); Fable 5 reserved for the hardest work. Extra tokens = 31% of the Fable-only bill, plus the $200/mo Max subscription.

epics.name: Platform Foundation, CI/CD & Quality
epics.description: Everything the product stands on, plus the quality system that keeps it honest: the Express/Prisma API (55-model schema), the iOS and web app shells, Railway production deploys, the four-app monorepo with unified CI/CD — and the pixel-diff compare tool (80+ web twins of iPhone components) plus a systematic iOS architecture audit and remediation.
deliverables: Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL API with 55-model schema
Railway production deploys, Docker, migration pipeline, build-once image promotion
Monorepo consolidation, OpenAPI docs, Postman collections, MCP dev tooling
Capture/compare tool: side-by-side screenshots + programmatic pixel diff, 80+ web twins
iOS audit remediation: typed navigation, error surfacing, 65 tests, lint gate

phases.name: Platform Foundation, Monorepo & CI/CD
phases.summary: Express + Prisma + PostgreSQL API with the 55-model schema, iOS and Laravel app shells, Railway production deploys with Docker and a migration pipeline, OpenAPI/Postman/MCP documentation, and the four-app monorepo with build-once image promotion.

phases.name: Quality Engineering: Parity Tooling & iOS Hardening
phases.summary: The quality system that makes cross-platform parity provable: a pixel-diff compare tool with 80+ web twins of iPhone components, plus a systematic iOS architecture audit and remediation — typed navigation, error surfacing, 65 tests, and a lint gate.

epics.name: Design Systems & Component Libraries
epics.description: The reusable UI foundation on both platforms: 122 SwiftUI components and a 52-component Blade/Vue library — including replatforming the entire web client onto Laravel in four days.
deliverables: 122-component SwiftUI system (cards, overlays, drag-reorder, animation framework)
Laravel replatform + 52-component Blade/Vue library (4 days, 108 commits)
PrimeVue admin design-system migration

phases.name: SwiftUI Component System
phases.summary: 122 custom components: cards, overlays, drag-reorder, and the animation framework that gives the iPhone app its native feel.

phases.name: Web Library & Laravel Replatform
phases.summary: Replatformed the entire web client onto Laravel in four days (108 commits), then built the 52-component Blade/Vue library and migrated the admin to the PrimeVue design system.

epics.name: Identity, Groups & Community
epics.description: Who you are and who you belong to: phone (SMS) verification for members, Google OAuth for leaders, organizations with role-based permissions — and the community model of groups with join codes, invitation and approval workflows, QR invites, posts and recurring events.
deliverables: Twilio SMS verification + consent + STOP webhook; Google OAuth with account linking
Member/organization model with RBAC roles and org-scoped content authorization
Group CRUD + 6-char join codes + approval workflow
QR/OG invite links + contact-picker invites; Posts and Events APIs

phases.name: Authentication, Orgs & Roles
phases.summary: Twilio SMS verification with consent + STOP webhook, Google OAuth with account linking, the member/organization model with RBAC roles, org-scoped content authorization, sliding sessions, and bot protection.

phases.name: Groups, Invites & Community Core
phases.summary: Groups with 6-character join codes and approval workflows, QR/OG invite links with contact-picker invites, and the Posts and Events APIs (recurrence, attendees).

epics.name: Curriculum, Lessons & Bible Platform
epics.description: The full content platform: leaders author complete study programs from their phone and enroll members with progress tracking; members consume themed lessons through rich players; five activity editors, a native Bible reader with AI semantic search, and the Cloudflare media/video pipeline feed it all.
deliverables: Program/lesson/schedule authoring, enrollment engine, progress + completion tracking
Slide-in editors for all 5 activity types; web lesson player with code-based themes
Native Bible reader with highlighting; pgvector semantic search + hybrid fusion + reranker
Cloudflare Stream video + R2 storage; in-app recorder with teleprompter
Claude AI enrichment: alt text, tags, media library with usage tracking

phases.name: Study Programs, Curriculum & Enrollment
phases.summary: Leaders author complete study programs from their phone: program/lesson/schedule authoring, the enrollment engine with overlap guardrails, progress and completion tracking, import/export, AI tag suggestions and covers.

phases.name: Lesson Experience & Activity Editors
phases.summary: The multi-block reading architecture with its theme system, slide-in editors for all five activity types with live preview, the web lesson player, exegesis highlighting, and the public preview player.

phases.name: Bible Platform (Reader, Search, AI)
phases.summary: A native Bible reader with search, translations and persistent highlighting, API.Bible integration with caching, and AI semantic search: pgvector embeddings + hybrid fusion + a reranker that answers plain-language questions with passages.

phases.name: Media & Video Pipeline
phases.summary: Cloudflare Stream video and R2 storage with responsive variants, the in-app video recorder with teleprompter, and Claude AI enrichment (alt text, tags) across the media library.

epics.name: Admin, Analytics & Engagement
epics.description: How leaders see and reach their people: the unified activity model with APNs push and deep links, the in-app notification feed and global search, and the full admin console — CRUD for every domain object plus a KPI analytics dashboard, built in one week.
deliverables: Unified Activity model + APNs push with deep links
In-app notification feed and global search
Admin shell with Pinia stores + API proxy; CRUD for all domains
ApexCharts KPI dashboard + virtualized member management

phases.name: Notifications, Search & Dashboards
phases.summary: The unified Activity model, APNs push with deep links, the in-app notification feed, global search, and the tappable KPI dashboard with the internal activity heatmap.

phases.name: Admin Console & Analytics
phases.summary: The full leader/admin console built in one week: admin shell with Pinia stores and the API proxy, CRUD for every domain object, the ApexCharts KPI dashboard, and virtualized member management.

epics.name: Member Web & Go-to-Market
epics.description: The member-facing web and the machinery to grow it: join a group by code with SMS verification, native-app-feel group and study homes, redesigned engagement views — plus carrier-compliant A2P SMS at scale, the legal surfaces, and the marketing site with the beta program.
deliverables: Public join flows (group/study/event) with keypad phone entry
Member group home, study home, profile; engagement analytics redesign
A2P SMS campaigns: templating, rate limiting, audit logging
Privacy/terms/opt-in pages + marketing website + Google-OAuth beta join

phases.name: Member Web Experience & Public Join
phases.summary: Join a group by code with SMS verification and keypad phone entry, native-app-feel member group home, study home and profile, the group switcher, study pager, and the redesigned engagement analytics.

phases.name: Compliance, SMS & Go-to-Market
phases.summary: Carrier-compliant A2P SMS campaigns with templating, rate limiting and audit logging; the privacy/terms/opt-in surfaces with consent carousel; and the marketing website with the Google-OAuth beta program.

epics.name: Web Platform Parity & Offline
epics.description: The web client reaches 100% parity with the iPhone app, then goes where the iPhone can’t: the active iPhone-to-web port (7 screens verified, 7 wired) completes all 27 tracked screens plus the Bible reader, and ships as an installable PWA with offline lesson caching for low-connectivity group settings.
deliverables: Verify the wired screens (groups chain, exegesis editor, video picker)
Member profile, requests, enrollment flow + scheduling
Calendar month grid, global search, notification feed, org home, Bible reader page
Installable PWA: offline shell, lesson caching with input sync, media prefetch

phases.name: iPhone-to-Web Parity Port
phases.summary: The remaining 20 of 27 tracked screens plus the Bible reader page — member profile, requests, enrollment flow and scheduling, calendar month grid, global search, notification feed, org home — each proven with a pixel diff against the iPhone original.

phases.name: Offline & PWA
phases.summary: The web client becomes installable: PWA with an offline shell, offline lesson caching with input sync on reconnect, and media prefetch for scheduled lessons — for members in low-connectivity group settings.

epics.name: Communication Platform
epics.description: Every way a group communicates, in two phases: multi-channel notifications (push, web push, email, two-way SMS) with per-user preferences, scheduling and delivery tracking — then group channels, DMs and mentions, whose WebSocket foundation Live Events extends into synchronized sessions. (16 work-weeks over ~18 calendar weeks spanning the holidays.)
deliverables: Multi-channel delivery: APNs + web push + email + SMS, per-user channel preferences
Two-way SMS: inbound handling, conversational replies, lesson reminders by text
Scheduled + digest notifications, templates, quiet hours, org-level triggers, A2P scale-up
Chat: group channels + DMs with mentions, per-conversation notification preferences
WebSocket real-time layer — the shared foundation for chat and live sessions

phases.name: Notifications & SMS Expansion
phases.summary: Multi-channel delivery — APNs, web push, email and two-way SMS — with per-user channel preferences, scheduled and digest notifications with templates, delivery tracking, quiet hours, org-level triggers, and A2P throughput scale-up.

phases.name: Group Chat
phases.summary: The largest unbuilt domain: group channels and direct messages with mentions, per-conversation notification preferences, push deep links, and moderation hooks — and it lays the WebSocket real-time foundation that Live Events extends.

epics.name: Creator Platform & Marketplace
epics.description: From single-leader authorship to a creator economy, in three phases: multi-user collaboration (invite anyone — including non-members — with per-resource permissions and an audit trail), the community marketplace (publish, discover, rate, adopt and version curriculum), and Stripe billing to monetize it. (29 work-weeks over ~32 calendar weeks spanning the holidays.)
deliverables: Collaborator invite lifecycle + per-resource permission grants enforced across ~115 endpoints
Permission editor + grant-aware editors on web and iPhone
Publish-to-community flow, discovery (browse/search/ratings), one-click adopt-for-my-group
Content versioning with update notifications; creator comments + messaging
Stripe subscription billing, usage tiers, marketplace payout rails

phases.name: Multi-User Collaboration
phases.summary: Invite anyone — including non-members — to contribute to programs, lessons and enrolled content: the invite lifecycle with account bootstrap, per-resource/per-action permission grants enforced across ~115 endpoints, permission editors on both clients, and a full audit trail.

phases.name: Community Marketplace
phases.summary: The app store for study programs: publish with review states, discovery with browse/search/categories/ratings, one-click adopt-for-my-group enrollment cloning, content versioning with update notifications, and creator-to-creator messaging.

phases.name: Billing & Monetization
phases.summary: Stripe subscription billing for organizations with usage-based tiers, and the payout rails that let marketplace creators earn.

epics.name: Enterprise Trust & Compliance
epics.description: Everything an organization needs to say yes: independent security testing and hardening, SSO with domain-open access (Google, Microsoft, generic OIDC), member data export/delete (GDPR/CCPA posture), AI-assisted content moderation, per-org white-labeling with self-serve onboarding, and a closing release-hardening pass.
deliverables: Penetration test + remediation, secrets rotation, backup drills, SOC 2 groundwork
OIDC SSO, domain verification, auto-provisioning with default roles
Personal data export + account/data deletion workflows, consent surfaces
AI content screening, org moderation policies, report/review workflows
Org brand kit + self-serve org creation; cross-feature regression + load testing

phases.name: Security Readiness
phases.summary: Penetration test and remediation, secrets rotation and production database network hardening, backup/restore drills, an incident runbook, and SOC 2 groundwork.

phases.name: Organization Access & SSO
phases.summary: Organizations toggle between invite-only and domain-open: OIDC sign-in (Google, Microsoft, generic), domain verification, auto-provisioning with default roles, and deprovisioning.

phases.name: Member Data Privacy Tooling
phases.summary: Full personal data export, account and data deletion workflows, and consent/retention surfaces — the GDPR/CCPA posture that AI insights requires before launch.

phases.name: Trust & Safety
phases.summary: AI-assisted content screening, org-level moderation policies with alerts, and report/review workflows — required before community content and chat scale.

phases.name: White-Labeling & Self-Serve Onboarding
phases.summary: Per-org brand kit (logo, colors, fonts) applied across member surfaces, self-serve org creation with a setup wizard, and org-level contact/social configuration.

phases.name: Hardening & Release
phases.summary: A closing stabilization pass: cross-feature regression, performance and load testing, and a security review.

epics.name: Analytics & AI Insights
epics.description: The data and AI layer: instrument every lesson interaction on both platforms (dwell time, drop-off, completion funnels, watch-time), feed all member input into an AI analysis pipeline for personal growth timelines and the Trophy Room — opt-in and member-owned — and extend the pgvector semantic-search stack to all content. (18 work-weeks over ~19 calendar weeks spanning the holidays.)
deliverables: Event pipeline: batched ingestion from web + iPhone players
Leader dashboards: cohort progress, struggle points, org engagement reporting
AI analysis pipeline: themes, sentiment, growth signals; Trophy Room on web + iPhone
Privacy opt-in controls wired to the data-ownership tooling
Semantic search across programs, lessons, notes and media

phases.name: Member Analytics
phases.summary: Event instrumentation across both lesson players: batched ingestion, per-activity dwell time, drop-off and completion funnels, video watch-time, cohort progress dashboards, and org-level engagement reporting.

phases.name: AI Growth Insights
phases.summary: All member input flows into an AI analysis pipeline — themes, sentiment, growth signals — producing personal growth timelines and the Trophy Room from the original vision, with opt-in, member-owned privacy controls.

phases.name: Universal Semantic Search
phases.summary: The pgvector/embedding/reranker stack built for Bible search extended to programs, lessons, notes and media — one search box that understands meaning, and sharper marketplace discovery.

epics.name: Habits & Accountability
epics.description: The recurring-cadence challenge and goal system — the densest unbuilt domain in the original spec (20 stories) and its strongest retention mechanic: per-period objectives and questions, leader solicitation, privacy-preserving progress sharing, and goals linked to challenge progress, all riding the Communication Platform’s reminders.
deliverables: Recurring frequency engine with per-period objectives and questions
Leader solicitation workflows; privacy-preserving progress sharing
Scheduled reminders via the Communication Platform
Goals with accountability sharing, linked to challenge progress
Templates, multimedia, curriculum integration

phases.name: Challenge Engine
phases.summary: The recurring frequency engine with per-period objectives and questions, challenge templates, multimedia, and curriculum integration.

phases.name: Solicitation & Reminders
phases.summary: Leader solicitation workflows, privacy-preserving progress sharing, and scheduled push reminders riding the Communication Platform.

phases.name: Goals & Accountability
phases.summary: Personal goals with accountability sharing, linked to challenge progress — completing original domains 7 and 8.

epics.name: Live Events
epics.description: The in-person gathering platform, released continuously as a background stream (16 work-weeks spread over ~10 calendar months) rather than one block: event scheduling with RSVP, QR check-in attendance, volunteer role assignments — then the headline feature: a live presentation mode where members join by QR code on their phones — no app install — and follow synchronized content, vote in polls, answer questionnaires and submit questions in real time, with a big-screen TV mode for the room. The real-time layer extends the WebSocket foundation Chat lays down.
deliverables: Event scheduling with recurrence, RSVP + attendee communication, ICS export, reminders
QR self check-in + leader roster attendance, with history feeding Member Analytics
Event roles: assign members to food, setup and activities with accept/decline + reminders
Live presentation sessions: join by QR on any phone, synchronized content and media, live polls, questionnaires and Q&A with real-time results
Big-screen (TV) mode: join code, synchronized presentation, live result visualizations

phases.name: Event Scheduling & RSVP
phases.summary: Completes the partially-built events APIs into full UX on both clients: create and schedule with recurrence, RSVP with attendee communication, ICS/calendar export, templates, and reminders riding the Communication Platform.

phases.name: Attendance & Event Roles
phases.summary: QR self check-in and leader roster check-in with attendance history (feeding Member Analytics later), plus volunteer coordination: leaders assign members to roles — food, setup, activities — with accept/decline and reminders.

phases.name: Real-Time Session Infrastructure
phases.summary: The live layer: WebSocket session and participant model, presenter state sync, and reconnection handling — extending the real-time foundation laid by Chat into synchronized group sessions.

phases.name: Live Presentation Mode
phases.summary: Leader-run sessions for in-person gatherings: members join by QR code on their phones — no app install required, so visitors participate too — and follow synchronized content and media, answer questionnaires, vote in live polls, and submit questions, with results aggregating in real time for the leader. Presentation content builds on the lesson players and activity types already shipped.

phases.name: Big-Screen (TV) Mode
phases.summary: A browser display surface for the room: join code up front, synchronized presentation content, and live poll/answer visualizations while members interact from their seats.
